ddls:
CREATE TABLE "cases" (
  "case_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each case */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date the case was reported */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the region where the case was reported */,
  "age_group_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the age group of the case */,
  "case_type" TEXT /* Type of case (e.g., new, cumulative) */,
  "case_count" INTEGER /* Number of cases */,
  "case_source" TEXT /* Source of the case (e.g., hospital, clinic) */,
  "case_status" TEXT /* Status of the case (e.g., active, recovered, deceased) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("case_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_cases_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_cases_age_group_id FOREIGN KEY ("age_group_id") REFERENCES age_groups ("age_group_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the region */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the region */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the region */,
  "latitude" TEXT /* Latitude of the region */,
  "longitude" TEXT /* Longitude of the region */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "age_groups" (
  "age_group_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each age group */,
  "age_group_name" TEXT /* Name of the age group */,
  "age_range" TEXT /* Age range of the group */,
  "age_range_start" INTEGER /* Start of the age range */,
  "age_range_end" INTEGER /* End of the age range */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("age_group_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "testing_data" (
  "test_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date the test was conducted */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the region where the test was conducted */,
  "test_type" TEXT /* Type of test (e.g., PCR, rapid) */,
  "test_count" INTEGER /* Number of tests */,
  "test_positive_count" INTEGER /* Number of positive tests */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("test_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_testing_data_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "vaccination_data" (
  "vaccination_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each vaccination */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date the vaccination was administered */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the region where the vaccination was administered */,
  "vaccination_type" TEXT /* Type of vaccination (e.g., Pfizer, Moderna) */,
  "vaccination_count" INTEGER /* Number of vaccinations */,
  "vaccination_dose_count" INTEGER /* Number of doses administered */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("vaccination_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_vaccination_data_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "demographics" (
  "demographic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each demographic entry */,
  "case_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the case */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the case */,
  "sex" TEXT /* Sex of the case */,
  "ethnicity" TEXT /* Ethnicity of the case */,
  "occupation" TEXT /* Occupation of the case */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("demographic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_demographics_case_id FOREIGN KEY ("case_id") REFERENCES cases ("case_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., public health official, administrator) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "data_accessed" TEXT /* Specific data accessed (e.g., cases, testing data) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "case_sequencing" (
  "sequencing_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sequencing entry */,
  "case_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the case */,
  "sequencing_date" TEXT /* Date the sequencing was performed */,
  "sequencing_count" INTEGER /* Number of sequences */,
  "sequencing_type" TEXT /* Type of sequencing (e.g., genomic, antibody) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sequencing_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_case_sequencing_case_id FOREIGN KEY ("case_id") REFERENCES cases ("case_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "case_presumptive" (
  "presumptive_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each presumptive entry */,
  "case_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the case */,
  "presumptive_date" TEXT /* Date the presumptive test was performed */,
  "presumptive_count" INTEGER /* Number of presumptive tests */,
  "presumptive_type" TEXT /* Type of presumptive test (e.g., rapid, PCR) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("presumptive_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_case_presumptive_case_id FOREIGN KEY ("case_id") REFERENCES cases ("case_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "screening_data" (
  "screening_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each screening entry */,
  "case_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the case */,
  "screening_date" TEXT /* Date the screening was performed */,
  "screening_count" INTEGER /* Number of screenings */,
  "screening_type" TEXT /* Type of screening (e.g., temperature, symptom) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("screening_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_screening_data_case_id FOREIGN KEY ("case_id") REFERENCES cases ("case_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "contact_tracing" (
  "contact_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each contact entry */,
  "case_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the case */,
  "contact_date" TEXT /* Date the contact was made */,
  "contact_count" INTEGER /* Number of contacts */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("contact_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_contact_tracing_case_id FOREIGN KEY ("case_id") REFERENCES cases ("case_id")
)
